page: Google Cloud Platform m1-megamem-96 is a memory-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs derived from 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with a massive 1434.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 14.93 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage for persistent data.

Benchmark results reveal a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the lower 25% tier, indicating modest individual core speeds. However, the server excels in parallelized tasks, with stress-ng multi-core performance and PassMark CPU scores ranking in the top 25%. Memory subsystem performance is exceptionally strong, with cached read bandwidth reaching 3918.5 GB/sec and uncached large-block write bandwidth at 89.97 GB/sec.

Given its high resource density, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require massive memory footprints rather than raw single-threaded CPU speed. It is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and memory-bound data processing pipelines. The trade-off involves lower single-threaded efficiency and the absence of local NVMe storage, making it ideal for workloads that scale horizontally across threads and leverage network-attached storage.
description: Google Cloud Platform m1-megamem-96 is a memory-optimized server designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor, it features 96 dedicated vCPUs across 48 physical cores and a massive 1434.0 GB of RAM, translating to 14.93 GB of memory per core. The server does not include local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks highlight strong multi-core capabilities and exceptional memory bandwidth, with cached read speeds reaching 3918.5 GB/sec, though single-core performance remains in the lower tier. This hardware profile makes the instance highly efficient for large-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-bound data processing, where massive RAM capacity and multi-threaded throughput are prioritized over single-threaded clock speeds.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m1-megamem-96 is a memory-optimized server featuring 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1.4 TB of RAM, designed for large-scale in-memory databases and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform m1-megamem-96 is a memory-optimized server with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1.4 TB of RAM, built for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring ninety-six dedicated vCPUs and over one terabyte of RAM for massive in-memory enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 48 physical cores
1434.0 GB of system memory providing 14.93 GB per core
Strong multi-core CPU and high-bandwidth memory performance
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for large-scale in-memory databases and caching layers
